step.title: Dispatch and Synthesize
step.goal: Execute the turn's composition: ask the user question (if any), dispatch subagents in parallel for the research agenda, perform the artifact checks, then synthesize all findings into a coherent set of turn-level facts.

check.ask:
Use the Question tool. Present session.user_question: - The question statement - Options list with RECOMMENDED FIRST (label ending in "(Recommended)") - Each option with a one-line description and trade-off
Capture the user's answer. Store as {{session.turn_user_answer}}: {answer, rejected_alternatives, why_not}.
The "why_not" for each rejected alternative is the agent's one-line reasoning, not the user's — the user picked one option, so the agent records WHY the others were on the table but rejected (this populates the Q&A log in step 5).

check.action: Skip this substep. Proceed to research dispatch.

substep.title: Load Research Dispatch Reference
substep.action: Load {{base_dir}}/references/subagent-dispatch.md to refresh the researcher-dispatch model and the web-approval gate.

check.ask:
One item in this turn's research agenda requires external/web research. Subagent: web-search-researcher. Topic: {{the web research item's question}}
Approve dispatching web search? - Approve (Recommended): proceed with web search - Decline: fall back to codebase + thoughts/ only; note deferral in questions file

check.action: Remove the web-search-researcher item from {{session.research_agenda}}. Note in {{session.turn_notes}} that web research was deferred this turn — step 5 will log this in the questions file.

substep.title: Dispatch researcher

invoke-agent.name: researcher

substep.action: Capture the returned synthesis as {{session.research_synthesis}}. researcher selects the children, dispatches them in parallel, validates and sharpens vague returns, and synthesizes — wait for its single return.

substep.title: Perform Artifact Checks
substep.action: For each item in session.artifact_checks, read the file/path directly using the read tool. Capture findings as part of the turn-level synthesis. These are unrestricted direct reads (bundle files, thoughts/ artifacts, root-level docs) per spec §7.2.

substep.title: Merge Turn Findings
substep.action:
Combine the turn's findings into a coherent block: - The user's answer (if any) -> direct quote or canonical rephrasing - researcher's research synthesis ({{session.research_synthesis}}) -> already synthesized and cited; fold it in as-is, do not re-summarize or paraphrase away its citations - Each artifact check finding -> one or two sentences with citation
The merged block is what gets written to the research file in step 5. It must read as factual findings — what IS — not opinions about what should change. Per QRDSPIV §Q↔R: "Research is factual. Gather FACTS, not opinions."
Store as {{session.turn_synthesis}}: a markdown block ready for appending to the research file.

substep.title: Spot Resolved Terms and Decision Triggers
substep.action:
Scan the turn's synthesis for:
- **Resolved terms**: did the user's answer or research clarify a term that should go into CONTEXT.md? Mark {{session.glossary_updates}} = list of {term, definition, aliases_to_avoid}.
- **Decision triggers**: did a hard-to-reverse decision surface this turn? Evaluate the three-condition gate from the decision-record-format skill: 1. Hard to reverse? 2. Surprising without context? 3. Result of a real trade-off?
If all three are true, mark {{session.dr_candidate}} = {title, context, decision, why, considered_options}. Step 5 will offer the DR to the user and scaffold if approved.
These flags drive step 5's bundle updates.

substep.title: Display Turn Findings

section.title: Research dispatch goes through researcher
section.description: All research dispatch goes through the researcher agent in a single dispatch per turn carrying the research-agenda batch. researcher handles child selection, parallel execution (Lock 28), validation, and synthesis internally. ticket-grill composes the questions and consumes the synthesis — it does not dispatch the research children directly.

rules.rule: One researcher dispatch per turn, carrying the whole research-agenda batch
Parallel child execution is researcher's concern, not ticket-grill's
One research question or four — still one researcher dispatch

section.title: Synthesis discipline
section.description:
Research findings in the bundle's research file are FACTUAL, evidence-bound, cited. They are not: - Opinions ("I think we should...") — opinions belong to ticket-design - Recommendations ("recommend Option A") — DRs and tickets carry these, not the research doc - Raw transcripts — paste from subagents loses signal in noise
A finding looks like: "Auth middleware in src/middleware/auth.ts:42 extracts user_id from JWT and attaches to req.user. All 38 route handlers under src/routes/ scope queries with req.user.id." Not: "It looks like the auth might work via JWT? The middleware seems to do something with user IDs."

rules.rule: A subagent return that says "no matches found" or "unable to determine" is a factual finding — capture it
"X does not exist in the codebase" is information; "we don't know if X exists" is not
If a subagent's prompt was bad and the return is unusable, redispatch with a sharper prompt — do not paper over the gap in synthesis

section.title: The DR three-condition gate is strict
section.description: Most turns produce zero DR candidates. Most decisions are easy to reverse, obvious, or had no real alternatives — they get logged in the Q&A file but do not warrant DRs. DRs are reserved for the load-bearing decisions; over-recording dilutes the signal. If in doubt, defer — the next turn or ticket-design phase can promote a Q&A entry to a DR if the gate truly fires.
